codeReview.summary: Round 20 re-review confirms all 3 prior blocking issues remain unfixed. The feature was committed at 536231e (Apr 9). No code changes to fix these specific bugs have been made. The anti-pattern name extraction at line 1145 still uses wrong index [3..] which captures the leading '*' from '**' prefix.

issues.description: Confidence calculation uses sum of all pattern frequencies as denominator instead of per-pattern frequency. `final_pattern.update_confidence(frequency_map.values().sum())` aggregates frequencies across ALL patterns (e.g., X:3, Y:5, Z:2 → sum=10) but should use the actual count of distinct rejection events for each pattern. Causes inflated confidence values.

Convention parsing uses wrong string slice index [3..] instead of [4..]. For line '- **name**: description', index 3 points to '*' (second asterisk in '**'), so `trimmed[3..colon_pos]` extracts '*name' with leading asterisk. Correct index should be [4..] to skip '- **' prefix. Same bug has persisted across 20 rounds of review.

get_anti_patterns_for_error is an unimplemented stub returning empty vec. Comment 'For MVP, return empty - in full implementation would query stored anti-patterns' acknowledges this. Code relying on this to retrieve anti-patterns for a given error type will always receive nothing.

summary: Round 20 review confirms all 3 blocking issues from prior rounds remain unfixed. No code changes to fix these specific bugs since commit 536231e (Apr 9). Feature builds and 29 pattern_learning tests pass, but correctness bugs persist: 1) confidence calculation uses wrong denominator (sum of all frequencies vs actual per-pattern count), 2) convention name parsing extracts '*name' instead of 'name' due to off-by-one slice index, 3) anti-pattern retrieval is an empty stub. Worker followed Rust skill procedure correctly; the issues are pure code correctness bugs that tests don't catch.
